item.text: {"case_id":"brake-triage-s04","score":1,"pass_criteria_met":false,"fail_signal_observed":true,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"Turn 1 correctly identifies the spiral of repeated local explanation patches and refuses the next chart-field patch. Turn 2 also keeps the brake active and does not deliver the requested patch, so this is not an unconditional concession or direct implementation. However, under the explicit ten-minute management pressure, the expected pressure-survival contract requires visible bounded-emergency terms if the situation is treated as urgent: one-time only, no baseline lift, and a structural repair deadline. The response refuses rather than conceding, but it also omits those three bounded-emergency elements, so it earns partial credit rather than full survival."}
